terms.name: Defense counters
terms.definition: The number of defense counters on a battle on the battlefield indicates how much defense it has. A battle with 0 defense is put into its owner’s graveyard if it isn’t the source of an ability that has triggered but not yet left the stack. This state-based action doesn’t use the stack. See rule 704.

terms.name: Effect Causing Object To Be Given counters
terms.definition: If an object enters the battlefield with counters on it, the effect causing the object to be given counters may specify which player puts those counters on it. If the effect doesn’t specify a player, the object’s controller puts those counters on it.
aliases: effect causing object to be given counters
Effect Causing Object To Be Given counters

terms.name: Effect Checks Object With counters
terms.definition: If a triggered ability instructs a player to put one object’s counters on another object and that ability’s trigger condition or effect checks that the object with those counters left the battlefield, the player doesn’t move counters from one object to the other. Rather, the player puts the same number of each kind of counter the first object had onto the second object. If the ability specifies what kind(s) of counters to place, the player puts the same number of each of those kinds of counter the first object had onto the second object.

terms.name: Instead Remove Shield counters
terms.definition: One or more shield counters on a permanent create a single replacement effect and a single prevention effect that protect the permanent. These effects are “If this permanent would be destroyed as the result of an effect, instead remove a shield counter from it” and “If damage would be dealt to this permanent, prevent that damage and remove a shield counter from it.” See rule 614, “Replacement Effects,” and rule 615, “Prevention Effects.”

terms.name: It Means To Remove counters
terms.definition: If an effect says to “move” a counter, it means to remove that counter from the object it’s currently on and put it onto a second object. If either of these actions isn’t possible, it’s not possible to move a counter, and no counter is removed from or put onto anything. This may occur if the first and second objects are the same object; if the first object doesn’t have the appropriate kind of counter on it; if the second object can’t have counters put onto it; or if either object is no longer in the correct zone.

terms.name: Keyword counters
terms.definition: A keyword counter on a permanent or on a card in a zone other than the battlefield causes that object to gain that keyword. The keywords that a keyword counter can be are flying, first strike, double strike, deathtouch, decayed, exalted, haste, hexproof, indestructible, lifelink, menace, reach, shadow, trample, and vigilance, as well as any variants of those keywords. See rule 613.1f.
terms.reminderText: don't reveal the second ability before the words have been chosen!

terms.name: Object Instructs Player To Its counters
terms.definition: If an activated ability of an object instructs a player to put its counters on another object and sacrificing the object with those counters is a cost to activate that ability, the player doesn’t move counters from one object to the other. Rather, the player puts the same number of each kind of counter the first object had onto the second object. If the ability specified what kind(s) of counters to place, the player puts the same number of each of those kinds of counters the first object had onto the second object.

terms.name: Prevent Damage Remove Shield counters
terms.definition: One or more shield counters on a permanent create a single replacement effect and a single prevention effect that protect the permanent. These effects are “If this permanent would be destroyed as the result of an effect, instead remove a shield counter from it” and “If damage would be dealt to this permanent, prevent that damage and remove a shield counter from it.” See rule 614, “Replacement Effects,” and rule 615, “Prevention Effects.”

terms.name: S Controller counters
terms.definition: If an object enters the battlefield with counters on it, the effect causing the object to be given counters may specify which player puts those counters on it. If the effect doesn’t specify a player, the object’s controller puts those counters on it.
terms.reminderText: Each clashing player reveals the top card of their library, then puts that card on their choice of the top or bottom. A player wins if their card had a greater mana value.

versions.definition: If an object enters the battlefield with counters on it, the effect causing the object to be given counters may specify which player puts those counters on it. If the effect doesn’t specify a player, the object’s controller puts those counters on it.

terms.name: S Given counters
terms.definition: Some spells and abilities refer to counters being put on an object. This refers to putting counters on that object while it’s on the battlefield and also to an object that’s given counters as it enters the battlefield.

terms.name: S Not Possible To Move counters
terms.definition: If an effect says to “move” a counter, it means to remove that counter from the object it’s currently on and put it onto a second object. If either of these actions isn’t possible, it’s not possible to move a counter, and no counter is removed from or put onto anything. This may occur if the first and second objects are the same object; if the first object doesn’t have the appropriate kind of counter on it; if the second object can’t have counters put onto it; or if either object is no longer in the correct zone.
aliases: s not possible to move counters
S Not Possible To Move counters

terms.name: Sacrificing Object With counters
terms.definition: If an activated ability of an object instructs a player to put its counters on another object and sacrificing the object with those counters is a cost to activate that ability, the player doesn’t move counters from one object to the other. Rather, the player puts the same number of each kind of counter the first object had onto the second object. If the ability specified what kind(s) of counters to place, the player puts the same number of each of those kinds of counters the first object had onto the second object.

terms.name: Same Of Kinds Of counters
terms.definition: If an activated ability of an object instructs a player to put its counters on another object and sacrificing the object with those counters is a cost to activate that ability, the player doesn’t move counters from one object to the other. terms.name: Shield counters
terms.definition: One or more shield counters on a permanent create a single replacement effect and a single prevention effect that protect the permanent. These effects are “If this permanent would be destroyed as the result of an effect, instead remove a shield counter from it” and “If damage would be dealt to this permanent, prevent that damage and remove a shield counter from it.” See rule 614, “Replacement Effects,” and rule 615, “Prevention Effects.”
terms.reminderText: If it would be dealt damage or destroyed, remove a shield counter from it instead.

terms.name: Token Is Not counters
terms.definition: A counter is a marker placed on an object or player that modifies its characteristics and/or interacts with a rule, ability, or effect. Counters are not objects and have no characteristics. Notably, a counter is not a token, and a token is not a counter. Counters with the same name or description are interchangeable.

terms.name: Absorb
terms.definition: A keyword ability that prevents damage. See rule 702.64, “Absorb.”
terms.reminderText: If a source would deal damage to a Sliver, prevent 1 of that damage.

versions.reminderText: If a source would deal damage to a Sliver, prevent 1 of that damage.

terms.name: Affinity
terms.definition: A keyword ability that reduces how much mana you need to spend to cast a spell. See rule 702.41, “Affinity.”
terms.reminderText: This card costs {1} less to cast for each permanent you control with affinity.

versions.reminderText: This card costs {1} less to cast for each permanent you control with affinity.

terms.name: Afflict
terms.definition: A keyword ability that makes the defending player lose life for blocking. See rule 702.130, “Afflict.”
terms.reminderText: Whenever this creature becomes blocked, defending player loses 2 life.

versions.reminderText: Whenever this creature becomes blocked, defending player loses 2 life.

terms.name: Afterlife
terms.definition: A keyword ability that leaves behind Spirit creature tokens when certain creatures die. See rule 702.135, “Afterlife.”
terms.reminderText: When this creature dies, create a 1/1 white and black Spirit creature token with flying.

versions.reminderText: When this creature dies, create a 1/1 white and black Spirit creature token with flying.

terms.name: Aftermath
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets a player cast one half of a split card only from their graveyard. See rule 702.127, “Aftermath.”
terms.reminderText: Cast this spell only from your graveyard. Then exile it.

versions.reminderText: Cast this spell only from your graveyard. Then exile it.

terms.name: Amplify
terms.definition: A keyword ability than can have a creature enter the battlefield with +1/+1 counters on it. See rule 702.38, “Amplify.”
terms.reminderText: As this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on it for each Zombie card you reveal in your hand.

versions.reminderText: As this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on it for each Zombie card you reveal in your hand.

terms.name: Annihilator
terms.definition: A keyword ability that can make a creature particularly brutal when it attacks. See rule 702.86, “Annihilator.”
terms.reminderText: Whenever this creature attacks, defending player sacrifices a permanent of their choice.

versions.reminderText: Whenever this creature attacks, defending player sacrifices a permanent of their choice.

terms.name: Ascend
terms.definition: A keyword causing a player to get the designation of the city’s blessing once they control ten permanents. See rule 702.131, “Ascend.”
terms.reminderText: If you control ten or more permanents, you get the city's blessing for the rest of the game.

versions.reminderText: If you control ten or more permanents, you get the city's blessing for the rest of the game.

terms.name: Assist
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets another player help you pay for a spell. See rule 702.132, “Assist.”
terms.reminderText: Another player can pay up to {6} of this spell's cost.

versions.reminderText: Another player can pay up to {6} of this spell's cost.

terms.name: Augment
terms.definition: A Magic keyword ability.
terms.reminderText: {2}, Reveal this card from your hand: Combine it with target host. Augment only as a sorcery.

versions.reminderText: {2}, Reveal this card from your hand: Combine it with target host. Augment only as a sorcery.

terms.name: Aura Swap
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets you exchange an Aura on the battlefield with one in your hand. See rule 702.65, “Aura Swap.”

terms.name: Awaken
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets you turn a land you control into a creature. See rule 702.113, “Awaken.”

terms.name: Backup
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets a creature give +1/+1 counters to itself or another when it enters the battlefield. If a different creature is chosen, that creature also temporarily gains one or more abilities. See rule 702.165, “Backup.”
terms.reminderText: When this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature. If that's another creature, it gains the following ability until end of turn.

versions.reminderText: When this creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature. If that's another creature, it gains the following ability until end of turn.

terms.name: Banding
terms.definition: Banding
terms.reminderText: Any creatures with banding, and up to one without, can attack in a band. Bands are blocked as a group. If any creatures with banding you control are blocking or being blocked by a creature, you divide that creature's combat damage, not its controller, among any of the creatures it's being blocked by or is blocking.

versions.reminderText: Any creatures with banding, and up to one without, can attack in a band. Bands are blocked as a group. If any creatures with banding you control are blocking or being blocked by a creature, you divide that creature's combat damage, not its controller, among any of the creatures it's being blocked by or is blocking.

terms.name: Bargain
terms.definition: Bargain is a keyword ability that represents an optional additional cost of sacrificing an artifact, enchantment, or token. A spell has been bargained if its controller declared the intention to pay that cost. See rule 702.166, “Bargain.”
terms.reminderText: You may sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as you cast this spell.

versions.reminderText: You may sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as you cast this spell.

terms.name: Basic landcycling
terms.definition: See Typecycling.
terms.reminderText: {2}, Discard this card: Search your library for a basic land card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.

terms.name: Battle Cry
terms.definition: A keyword ability that makes other attacking creatures better in combat. See rule 702.91, “Battle Cry.”
terms.reminderText: Whenever this creature attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.

versions.reminderText: Whenever this creature attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.

terms.name: Bestow
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets a creature card be cast as an Aura. See rule 702.103, “Bestow.”

terms.name: Blitz
terms.definition: A keyword ability found on creature cards that allows them to be cast for an alternative cost. See rule 702.152, “Blitz.”
terms.reminderText: If you cast a spell for its blitz cost, it gains haste and "When this creature dies, draw a card." Sacrifice it at the beginning of the next end step.

versions.reminderText: If you cast a spell for its blitz cost, it gains haste and "When this creature dies, draw a card." Sacrifice it at the beginning of the next end step.

terms.name: Bloodthirst
terms.definition: A keyword ability that can have a creature enter the battlefield with +1/+1 counters on it. See rule 702.54, “Bloodthirst.”
terms.reminderText: If an opponent was dealt damage this turn, this creature enters with a +1/+1 counter on it.

versions.reminderText: If an opponent was dealt damage this turn, this creature enters with a +1/+1 counter on it.

terms.name: Boast
terms.definition: A special kind of activated ability that can be activated only once each turn if the creature with the boast ability attacked that turn. See rule 702.142, “Boast.”

terms.name: Bushido
terms.definition: A keyword ability that can make a creature better in combat. See rule 702.45, “Bushido.”
terms.reminderText: Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn.

versions.reminderText: Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn.

terms.name: Buyback
terms.definition: A keyword ability of instants and sorceries that can let the spell return to its owner’s hand as it resolves. See rule 702.27, “Buyback.”
terms.reminderText: You may pay an additional {3} as you cast this spell. If you do, put this card into your hand as it resolves.

versions.reminderText: You may pay an additional {3} as you cast this spell. If you do, put this card into your hand as it resolves.

terms.name: Cascade
terms.definition: A keyword ability that may let a player cast a random extra spell for no cost. See rule 702.85, “Cascade.”
terms.reminderText: When you cast this spell, exile cards from the top of your library until you exile a nonland card that costs less. You may cast it without paying its mana cost. Put the exiled cards on the bottom in a random order.

versions.reminderText: When you cast this spell, exile cards from the top of your library until you exile a nonland card that costs less. You may cast it without paying its mana cost. Put the exiled cards on the bottom in a random order.

terms.name: Casualty
terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows you to sacrifice a creature to create a copy of a spell. See 702.153, “Casualty.”
terms.reminderText: As you cast this spell, you may sacrifice a creature with power 1 or greater. When you do, copy this spell and you may choose a new target for the copy.

versions.reminderText: As you cast this spell, you may sacrifice a creature with power 1 or greater. When you do, copy this spell and you may choose a new target for the copy.

terms.name: Champion
terms.definition: Champion

terms.name: Changeling
terms.definition: A characteristic-defining ability that grants the object it’s on every creature type. See rule 702.73, “Changeling.”
terms.reminderText: This card is every creature type.

versions.reminderText: This card is every creature type.

terms.name: Choose a background
terms.definition: A variant of the partner ability that lets two legendary permanent cards be your commander in the Commander variant rather than one if one of them has the “choose a Background” ability and the other is a Background enchantment card. See rule 702.124, “Partner,” and rule 903, “Commander.”
terms.reminderText: You can have a Background as a second commander.

versions.definition: A variant of the partner ability that lets two legendary permanent cards be your commander in the Commander variant rather than one if one of them has the “choose a Background” ability and the other is a Background enchantment card. See rule 702.124, “Partner,” and rule 903, “Commander.”

A variant of the partner ability that lets two legendary permanent cards be your commander in the Commander variant rather than one if one of them has the “choose a Background” ability and the other is a Background enchantment card. See rule 702.124, “Partner,” and rule 903, “Commander.”

A variant of the partner ability that lets two legendary permanent cards be your commander in the Commander variant rather than one if one of them has the “choose a Background” ability and the other is a Background enchantment card. See rule 702.124, “Partner,” and rule 903, “Commander.”

terms.name: Cipher
terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows you to encode a card on a creature and cast that card whenever that creature deals combat damage to a player. See rule 702.99, “Cipher.”
terms.reminderText: Then you may exile this spell card encoded on a creature you control. Whenever that creature deals combat damage to a player, its controller may cast a copy of the encoded card without paying its mana cost.

versions.reminderText: Then you may exile this spell card encoded on a creature you control. Whenever that creature deals combat damage to a player, its controller may cast a copy of the encoded card without paying its mana cost.

terms.name: Cleave
terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows you to pay an alternative cost to remove some of a spell’s text. See rule 702.148, “Cleave.”

terms.name: Commander ninjutsu
terms.definition: A variant of the ninjutsu ability. See rule 702.49, “Ninjutsu.”

terms.name: Companion
terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows a player to choose one creature card from outside the game as a companion if the restriction of that card’s companion ability is met. Once a player has chosen a companion, that player may pay {3} to put it into their hand once during the game. See rule 702.139, “Companion.”
terms.reminderText: You can have two commanders if the other is the Doctor.

versions.reminderText: You can have two commanders if the other is the Doctor.

terms.name: Compleated
terms.definition: A keyword ability that causes a planeswalker to enter the battlefield with fewer loyalty counters if a player chose to pay life for Phyrexian mana symbols in its cost. See rule 702.150, “Compleated.”
terms.reminderText: {B/P} can be paid with {B} or 2 life. If life was paid, this planeswalker enters with two fewer loyalty counters.

versions.reminderText: {B/P} can be paid with {B} or 2 life. If life was paid, this planeswalker enters with two fewer loyalty counters.

terms.name: Conspire
terms.definition: A keyword ability that creates a copy of a spell. See rule 702.78, “Conspire.”
terms.reminderText: As you cast this spell, you may tap two untapped creatures you control that share a color with it. When you do, copy it and you may choose a new target for the copy.

versions.reminderText: As you cast this spell, you may tap two untapped creatures you control that share a color with it. When you do, copy it and you may choose a new target for the copy.

terms.name: Convoke
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets you tap creatures rather than pay mana to cast a spell. See rule 702.51, “Convoke.”
terms.reminderText: Your creatures can help cast this spell. Each creature you tap while casting this spell pays for {1} or one mana of that creature's color.

versions.reminderText: Your creatures can help cast this spell. Each creature you tap while casting this spell pays for {1} or one mana of that creature's color.

terms.name: Craft
terms.definition: Craft is an activated ability that allows a player to exile cards from their graveyard and/or permanents they control to exile the permanent with the craft ability and return it onto the battlefield transformed. See rule 702.167, “Craft.”

terms.name: Crew
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets you tap creatures to turn a Vehicle into an artifact creature. See rule 301, “Artifacts,” and rule 702.122, “Crew.”
terms.reminderText: Tap any number of creatures you control with total power 1 or more: This Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.

versions.definition: Tap creatures with total power N or more: this Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.

terms.name: Cumulative upkeep
terms.definition: A keyword ability that imposes an increasing cost to keep a permanent on the battlefield. See rule 702.24, “Cumulative Upkeep.”

terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows certain spells to be cast for an alternative cost if an opponent has lost life. See rule 702.137, “Spectacle.”

terms.name: Splice
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets a player add a card’s rules text onto another spell. See rule 702.47, “Splice.”

terms.name: Split second
terms.definition: A keyword ability that makes it nearly impossible for a player to respond to a spell. See rule 702.61, “Split Second.”
terms.reminderText: As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.

versions.reminderText: As long as this spell is on the stack, players can't cast spells or activate abilities that aren't mana abilities.

terms.name: Spree
terms.definition: An ability found on some modal spells that allows you to choose one or more modes and requires you to pay additional costs for those modes. See rule 702.172, “Spree.”
terms.reminderText: Choose one or more additional costs.

versions.reminderText: Choose one or more additional costs.

terms.name: Squad
terms.definition: A keyword ability that creates copies of a creature when it enters the battlefield. See rule 702.157, “Squad.”
terms.reminderText: As an additional cost to cast this spell, you may pay {2} any number of times. When this creature enters, create that many tokens that are copies of it.

versions.reminderText: As an additional cost to cast this spell, you may pay {2} any number of times. When this creature enters, create that many tokens that are copies of it.

terms.name: Station
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets you tap creatures to add charge counters to station cards. See rule 702.184, “Station,” and rule 721, “Station Cards.”
terms.reminderText: Tap another creature you control: Put charge counters equal to its power on this Planet. Station only as a sorcery.

versions.reminderText: Tap another creature you control: Put charge counters equal to its power on this Planet. Station only as a sorcery.

terms.name: Storm
terms.definition: A keyword ability that creates copies of a spell. See rule 702.40, “Storm.”
terms.reminderText: When you cast this spell, copy it for each spell cast before it this turn. You may choose new targets for the copies.

versions.reminderText: When you cast this spell, copy it for each spell cast before it this turn. You may choose new targets for the copies.

terms.name: Sunburst
terms.definition: A keyword ability that can have a permanent enter the battlefield with +1/+1 counters or charge counters on it. See rule 702.44, “Sunburst.”
terms.reminderText: This creature enters with a +1/+1 counter on it for each color of mana spent to cast it.

versions.reminderText: This creature enters with a +1/+1 counter on it for each color of mana spent to cast it.

terms.name: Surge
terms.definition: A keyword ability that provides an alternative cost to cast a card if you or one of your teammates has cast another spell in the same turn. See rule 702.117, “Surge.”

terms.name: Suspend
terms.definition: A keyword ability that provides an alternative way to play a card. See rule 702.62, “Suspend.” A card is “suspended” if it’s in the exile zone, has suspend, and has a time counter on it.

terms.name: Swampcycling
terms.definition: See Typecycling.
terms.reminderText: {2}, Discard this card: Search your library for a Swamp card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.

terms.name: Swampwalk
terms.definition: See Landwalk.
terms.reminderText: This creature can't be blocked as long as defending player controls a Swamp.

terms.name: Tiered
terms.definition: A keyword ability found on some modal spells that requires you to pay an additional cost for the mode you choose. See rule 702.183, “Tiered.”

terms.name: Toxic

versions.reminderText: Players dealt combat damage by this creature also get a poison counter.

terms.name: Training

terms.name: Trample

terms.name: Transmute

terms.name: Tribute

versions.reminderText: As this creature enters, an opponent of your choice may put two +1/+1 counters on it.

terms.name: Typecycling
terms.definition: A variant of the cycling ability. See rule 702.29, “Cycling.”

versions.definition: A variant of the cycling ability. See rule 702.29, “Cycling.”

terms.name: Umbra armor
terms.definition: A keyword ability that allows an Aura to protect the permanent it’s enchanting. See rule 702.89, “Umbra Armor.”

versions.reminderText: If enchanted creature would be destroyed, instead remove all damage from it and destroy this Aura.

terms.name: Undaunted
terms.definition: A keyword ability that reduces the cost of a spell based on the number of opponents you have. See rule 702.125, “Undaunted.”

terms.name: Undying
terms.definition: A keyword ability that can return a creature from the graveyard to the battlefield. See rule 702.93, “Undying.”
terms.reminderText: When this creature dies, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a +1/+1 counter on it.

versions.reminderText: When this creature dies, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a +1/+1 counter on it.

terms.name: Unearth

terms.reminderText: {2}: Return this card from your graveyard to the battlefield. It gains haste. Exile it at the beginning of the next end step or if it would leave the battlefield. Unearth only as a sorcery.

terms.definition: To take a card from where it is (usually the hand), put it on the stack, and pay its costs, so that it will eventually resolve and have its effect. See rule 601, “Casting Spells.”

terms.name: Cloak

terms.reminderText: Put it onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature with ward {2}. Turn it face up any time for its mana cost if it's a creature card.

terms.name: Collect evidence
terms.definition: A keyword action. To “collect evidence N” means to exile any number of cards from your graveyard with total mana value N or greater. See rule 701.59, “Collect Evidence.”

terms.name: Convert
terms.definition: To turn a double-faced card so its other face is up. See rule 701.28, “Convert.”

terms.name: Earthbend
terms.definition: A keyword action that causes a land to become a 0/0 creature with haste in addition to its other types and puts a number of +1/+1 counters on it. When that land dies or is put into exile, return it to the battlefield tapped under your control. See 701.66, “Earthbend.”

terms.name: Endure

terms.name: Exchange

terms.name: Exert

terms.name: Exile

See rule 406, “Exile.”

terms.name: Manifest
terms.definition: A keyword action that puts a card onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature. See rule 701.40, “Manifest,” and rule 708, “Face-Down Spells and Permanents.”
terms.reminderText: Put that card onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature. Turn it face up any time for its mana cost if it's a creature card.

terms.name: Manifest dread
terms.definition: A keyword action that puts one of the top two cards of your library onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature. See rule 701.62, “Manifest Dread,” rule 701.40, “Manifest,” and rule 708, “Face-Down Spells and Permanents.”
terms.reminderText: Look at the top two cards of your library. Put one onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature and the other into your graveyard. Turn it face up any time for its mana cost if it's a creature card.

versions.reminderText: Look at the top two cards of your library. Put one onto the battlefield face down as a 2/2 creature and the other into your graveyard. Turn it face up any time for its mana cost if it's a creature card.

terms.name: Meld
terms.definition: To turn two members of a meld pair so their back faces are up and combined into one oversized Magic card. See rule 701.42, “Meld.”

terms.name: Mill
terms.definition: To mill a number of cards, a player puts that many cards from the top of their library into their graveyard. See rule 701.17.
terms.reminderText: To amass Orcs X, put X +1/+1 counters on an Army you control. It's also an Orc. If you don't control an Army, create a 0/0 black Orc Army creature token first.

terms.name: Monstrosity
terms.definition: A keyword action that puts +1/+1 counters on a creature and makes it become monstrous. See rule 701.37, “Monstrosity.”
terms.reminderText: If this creature isn't monstrous, put three +1/+1 counters on it and it becomes monstrous.

versions.reminderText: If this creature isn't monstrous, put three +1/+1 counters on it and it becomes monstrous.

terms.name: Open an Attraction
terms.definition: Open an Attraction
terms.reminderText: Put the top card of your Attraction deck onto the battlefield.

versions.reminderText: Put the top card of your Attraction deck onto the battlefield.

terms.name: Planeswalk
terms.definition: To put each face-up plane card or phenomenon card on the bottom of its owner’s planar deck face down, then move the top card of your planar deck off that planar deck and turn it face up in a Planechase game. See rule 701.31, “Planeswalk.”
terms.reminderText: If you're playing Planechase, proceed to the next plane.

versions.reminderText: If you're playing Planechase, proceed to the next plane.

terms.name: Play
terms.definition:
1. To play a land is to put a land onto the battlefield as a special action. See rule 116, “Special Actions,” and rule 305, “Lands.”
2. To play a card is to play that card as a land or cast that card as a spell, whichever is appropriate. See rule 601, “Casting Spells.”
3. (Obsolete) Casting a spell used to be known as playing a spell. Cards with that text have received errata in the Oracle card reference. See Cast.
4. (Obsolete) Activating an activated ability used to be known as playing an activated ability. Cards with that text have received errata in the Oracle card reference. See Activate.
5. (Obsolete) The battlefield used to be known as the in-play zone. Cards that were printed with text that contains the phrases “in play,” “from play,” “into play,” or the like are referring to the battlefield and have received errata in the Oracle card reference. See Battlefield.
terms.reminderText: Mana cost includes color.

terms.name: Plot
terms.definition: A keyword ability that lets a player exile cards from their hand and cast them without paying their mana cost on future turns. See rule 702.170, “Plot.”

terms.name: Populate
terms.definition: A keyword action that creates a copy of a creature token you control. See rule 701.36, “Populate.”
terms.reminderText: Create a token that's a copy of a creature token you control.

versions.reminderText: Create a token that's a copy of a creature token you control.

terms.name: Prepared
terms.definition: A designation a permanent may have that allows you to cast a copy of that card with its alternative characteristics. See rule 722, “Preparation Cards.”
terms.reminderText: While it's prepared, you may cast a copy of its spell. Doing so unprepares it.

versions.definition: A designation a permanent may have that allows you to cast a copy of that card with its alternative characteristics. See rule 722, “Preparation Cards.”

terms.name: Proliferate
terms.definition: To give an additional counter to any number of players and/or permanents of each kind they already have. See rule 701.34, “Proliferate.”
terms.reminderText: Choose any number of permanents and/or players, then give each another counter of each kind already there.

terms.name: Regenerate
terms.definition: To replace a permanent’s destruction with an alternate sequence of events. See rule 701.19, “Regenerate.”

terms.name: Reveal
terms.definition: To show a card to all players for a brief time. See rule 701.20, “Reveal.”
terms.reminderText: Mana base cards add mana. Removal cards are instants and sorceries that can damage, destroy, or exile opposing permanents. Win conditions cost 6 or more mana.

terms.definition: Roll to Visit Your Attractions

terms.name: Sacrifice
terms.definition: To move a permanent you control to its owner’s graveyard. See rule 701.21, “Sacrifice.”
terms.reminderText: Damage and effects that say "destroy" don't destroy it.

terms.name: Scry
terms.definition: To manipulate some of the cards on top of your library. See rule 701.22, “Scry.”
terms.reminderText: Look at the top card of your library. You may put that card on the bottom.

versions.definition: Look at the top N cards of your library; put any number on the bottom and the rest on top in any order.

terms.name: Seek
terms.definition: A Magic keyword action.

terms.name: Set in motion
terms.definition: To move a scheme card off the top of your scheme deck and turn it face up. See rule 701.32, “Set in Motion.”

terms.name: Shuffle
terms.definition: To randomize the cards in a deck (before a game) or library (during a game). See rule 103.3 and rule 701.24.
terms.reminderText: A legendary team-up creature is two or more legendary characters teamed up on the same legendary creature card, such as Drana and Linvala.

versions.reminderText: A legendary team-up creature is two or more legendary characters teamed up on the same legendary creature card, such as Drana and Linvala.

terms.name: Support
terms.definition: A keyword action that lets you put +1/+1 counters on creatures. See rule 701.41, “Support.”
terms.reminderText: Put a +1/+1 counter on each of up to two target creatures.

versions.reminderText: Put a +1/+1 counter on each of up to two target creatures.

terms.name: Surveil
terms.definition: To manipulate some of the cards on top of your library, sending some of them to your graveyard and rearranging the rest. See rule 701.25, “Surveil.”
terms.reminderText: Look at the top card of your library. You may put it into your graveyard.

versions.reminderText: Look at the top card of your library. You may put it into your graveyard.

terms.name: Suspect
terms.definition: Suspect

terms.name: Tap
terms.definition: To turn a permanent sideways from an upright position. See rule 701.26, “Tap and Untap.”

terms.name: Time Travel
terms.definition: To add a time counter to or remove a time counter from any number of permanents you control with a time counter on them or suspended cards you own in exile with time counters on them. See rule 701.56, “Time Travel.”
terms.reminderText: For each suspended card you own and each permanent you control with a time counter on it, you may add or remove a time counter.

versions.reminderText: For each suspended card you own and each permanent you control with a time counter on it, you may add or remove a time counter.

terms.name: Transform
terms.definition: To turn a double-faced card so its other face is up. See rule 701.27, “Transform.”

terms.name: Treasure
terms.definition: A Magic keyword action.
terms.reminderText: It's an artifact with "{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color."

versions.reminderText: It's an artifact with "{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color."

terms.name: Triple
terms.definition: A keyword action that increases a creature’s power and/or toughness. See rule 701.11, “Triple.”

terms.name: Untap
terms.definition: To rotate a permanent back to the upright position from a sideways position. See rule 701.26, “Tap and Untap.”

terms.name: Venture into the dungeon
terms.definition: A keyword action that can bring dungeon cards into the game from outside the game or move a player’s venture marker. See rule 701.49, “Venture into the Dungeon.”
terms.reminderText: Enter the first room or advance to the next room.

terms.reminderText: While paying a waterbend cost, you can tap your artifacts and creatures to help. Each one pays for {1}.

terms.name: Role
terms.definition: Roles are colorless enchantment tokens. Each one has the Aura and Role subtypes and the enchant creature ability. If a permanent has more than one Role attached to it controlled by the same player, each of those Roles except the one with the most recent timestamp is put into its owner’s graveyard. See rule 303.7 and rules 111.10j–r.
